questions.question: What is the person doing in the snow, and what equipment are they using?
questions.answer_key: Person is skiing or snowboarding down a snowy slope with visible skis or board.
questions.rationale: High-need window 14.9–16.4s: core action defining the entire clip activity.

questions.question: What color is the main equipment visible in the frames?
questions.answer_key: Blue skis or snowboard visible against white snow.
questions.rationale: Helps listener visualize the contrast and identify the equipment clearly.

questions.question: What is the setting or terrain where this activity is taking place?
questions.answer_key: A snowy mountain slope or ski run covered in white snow.
questions.rationale: High-need window 11.9–14.9s: establishes location context essential for understanding scene.

questions.question: What visual pattern or marks are visible on the snow surface?
questions.answer_key: Tracks or lines visible in the snow from skiing/snowboarding movement.
questions.rationale: Indicates motion and terrain texture; helps blind listener understand slope conditions.

questions.question: What is the perspective or camera angle showing in these frames?
questions.answer_key: First-person or close overhead view of skier/snowboarder moving down slope.
questions.rationale: High-need window 8.9–11.9s: perspective is essential for spatial understanding of action.
